summary: Added a deterministic localization review export workflow and generated reviewer-friendly CSV/JSON packets from current Localizable.strings and Localizable.stringsdict resources without replacing translations or changing app resources.

contract_status_changes.before: The repo defined translation-quality status labels and review expectations, but future reviewers had no repo-native CSV/JSON packet generated from the current resources.
contract_status_changes.after: Tools/localization-review-export.py exports current English source values, plural entries, locale values, and status labels into localization/review/translation-review-export.csv and .json.

contract_status_changes.before: The placeholder baseline was documented, but reviewers could not filter current locale values by placeholder/draft/source status in a maintained export.
contract_status_changes.after: The export marks English rows as english-source and marks 5760 current non-English review rows as english-placeholder, including plural review entries.

contract_status_changes.before: The translation-quality workflow existed, but the generated packet was not linked from the docs map or validation workflow.
contract_status_changes.after: docs/README.md, docs/workflows/localization-translation-quality.md, and docs/workflows/validation.md point future agents to the export command and generated review packet.

residual_risks: No non-English translations were replaced in this slice.
No native or fluent reviewer has accepted any non-English locale values.
The export is a snapshot; regenerate it before consuming reviewer changes in a later replacement slice.
Runtime locale smoke, screenshot proof, device proof, and TestFlight proof were not changed by this export-only slice.
recommended_next_reason: After a native/fluent reviewer returns accepted values for one locale or small language family, ingest that reviewed packet in a narrow replacement slice while preserving parity, status labels, and localization validation.
